This action will be a Firm Fixed-Price Contract to include software and associated success support with an estimated annual cost of $532,194 for a one (1) year base period with four (4) one-year option periods. The provider is a HUBZone small business set-aside directed to DH Technologies, LLC (CAGE: 6XKC9), 161 Fort Evans Road NE, Suite 255 Leesburg VA 20176.

The solicitation proposes to award an Indefinite Quantity Contract (IQC) for:
• Posit Package Manager Advanced
• Posit Connect Advanced - 200 Licenses Package
• Posit Workbench Advanced - 20 Licenses Package
• Posit Success Support

DH Technologies, LLC (CAGE: 6XKC9) is the only HUBZone small business source that is a Posit authorized distributor identified through market research that is capable of providing these part numbers. The contract will cover a Base Period of one year with four one year option periods for each item. The total estimated dollar value for the base year is $532,194.

The Government will make award to one HUBZone small business.

L.3.1. Exceptions: Offerors are required to meet all solicitation requirements, such as terms and conditions, representations and certifications, and technical requirements, in addition to those identified as evaluation factors. Failure to meet a requirement may result in an offer being ineligible for award. If the offeror finds it necessary to take exception to any of the requirements specified in this solicitation, the offeror shall clearly identify the applicable Volume and exceptions with a complete explanation of why the exception was taken, what benefit accrues to the Government (if any), and its impact, if any, on the performance, schedule, price, and specific requirements of the solicitation. Each exception shall be specifically related to each paragraph and/or specific part of the solicitation to which the exception is taken. Offerors are cautioned that taking an exception may render the offer ineligible for award.

IAW FAR 12 The Government intends to make a source selection as part of a HUBZone competitive set-aside utilizing the Lowest Price Technically Acceptable (LPTA) process. The LPTA process does not permit tradeoffs among price and non-price factors. Offerors shall assume that the Government has no prior knowledge of their facilities and experience. The Government will base its evaluation on the information presented in the Offeror's proposal.
Factor 1. Management/Technical Approach.
The Government will evaluate the offeror's management approach to determine if the proposed approach demonstrates an understanding of the need for a highly qualified, stable contractor workforce and will result in a highly qualified, stable contractor workforce capable of performing awarded TO requirements.
The Government will evaluate the offeror's Technical Approach to determine if the offeror has demonstrated the ability to successfully perform and a clear understanding of the PWS Task Areas in the OPI field.
The Government will evaluate the offeror's proposed Staffing and Management Approach to determine whether the offeror has proposed a comprehensive approach to plan, execute, and manage a task order and to provide qualified key personnel.

The clause at 52.219-3, Notice of HUBZone Set-Aside or Sole-Source Award, is incorporated by reference into this contract. This clause applies to contracts that have been set aside for HUBZone small business concerns or awarded on a sole-source basis to a HUBZone small business concern. The Limitations on Subcontracting clause at 52.219-14 also applies, referencing contracts using the HUBZone price evaluation preference to award to a HUBZone small business concern unless the concern waived the evaluation preference.

SECTION I – CONTRACT CLAUSES

The following Federal Acquisition Regulation (FAR) clauses are incorporated by reference and apply to this solicitation and any resulting contract:

52.219-3 Notice of HUBZone Set-Aside or Sole-Source Award (NOV 2020)
52.219-4 Notice of Price Evaluation Preference for HUBZone Small Business Concerns (OCT 2022)
52.219-8 Utilization of Small Business Concerns (OCT 2022)
52.219-14 Limitations on Subcontracting (OCT 2022)
52.219-28 Postaward Small Business Program Rerepresentation (MAR 2023)

This solicitation is issued as a total HUBZone small business set-aside pursuant to FAR 19.1305. Only offers from HUBZone small business concerns that are certified by the U.S. Small Business Administration (SBA) at the time of offer submission and that maintain their HUBZone certification through contract award will be considered for award. Offerors must be listed as a certified HUBZone small business concern in the SBA's Dynamic Small Business Search (DSBS) at the time of initial offer and at the time of award.

The applicable NAICS code for this requirement is 561210 – Facilities Support Services, with a size standard of $47.0 million. The HUBZone price evaluation preference factor of 10 percent shall apply in accordance with FAR 19.1307 when evaluating offers from HUBZone small business concerns against offers from other than HUBZone small business concerns, should the set-aside be withdrawn. Contractors must comply with FAR 52.219-14, Limitations on Subcontracting, ensuring that at least 50 percent of the cost of contract performance incurred for personnel shall be expended for employees of the HUBZone concern.

The U.S. Department of Agriculture, Agricultural Research Service, Southeast Area, anticipates awarding a single Firm Fixed-Price contract for facilities maintenance and custodial support services at the Jamie Whitten Delta States Research Center in Stoneville, Mississippi. The period of performance includes a 12-month base period and four 12-month option periods.

SOLICITATION NUMBER: W912EP-25-R-0043
ISSUING OFFICE: U.S. Army Corps of Engineers, Jacksonville District
SET-ASIDE: Total HUBZone Small Business Set-Aside

1.0 DESCRIPTION

The U.S. Army Corps of Engineers (USACE), Jacksonville District, is soliciting proposals from qualified HUBZone certified small business concerns for environmental remediation and restoration services at multiple Formerly Used Defense Sites (FUDS) throughout Puerto Rico and the U.S. Virgin Islands. This requirement is set aside exclusively for HUBZone small business concerns in accordance with FAR Subpart 19.13 and DFARS 219.13.

The contracting officer has conducted market research in accordance with FAR Part 10 and has determined there is a reasonable expectation that at least two responsible HUBZone small business concerns will submit offers at fair market prices. This determination supports the total HUBZone set-aside pursuant to FAR 19.1305(b).

2.0 CONTRACT TYPE AND ESTIMATED VALUE

The Government intends to award a single Indefinite-Delivery/Indefinite-Quantity (IDIQ) contract with a guaranteed minimum of $250,000 and a maximum ceiling of $22,000,000 over a five-year ordering period. The applicable NAICS code is 562910 – Remediation Services, with a size standard of $25.0 million. Individual task orders will be issued on a Firm Fixed-Price or Cost-Plus-Fixed-Fee basis depending on the nature of the work.

3.0 ELIGIBILITY

To be eligible for award, offerors must be certified as a HUBZone small business concern by the SBA at the time of offer submission and must represent their HUBZone status in the System for Award Management (SAM.gov). The clause at FAR 52.219-3, Notice of HUBZone Set-Aside or Sole-Source Award, is incorporated herein. Failure to maintain HUBZone certification may result in disqualification from award consideration.

JUSTIFICATION FOR OTHER THAN FULL AND OPEN COMPETITION
HUBZone Sole Source Award

1. Agency: Department of Veterans Affairs, Network Contracting Office 4, Pittsburgh, PA
2. Contracting Activity: Veterans Health Administration, VA Pittsburgh Healthcare System
3. Description of Action: Sole source award to a HUBZone small business concern for biomedical equipment preventive maintenance and repair services.
4. Authority: FAR 6.302-5(b)(5) – Set-asides for HUBZone small business concerns authorized under FAR 19.1306.

5. Basis for Sole Source Justification:

Pursuant to FAR 19.1306(a), a sole source HUBZone award may be made when the contracting officer does not have a reasonable expectation that offers would be received from two or more HUBZone small business concerns. Market research, including review of SAM.gov registrations, SBA's HUBZone certification database, and prior procurement history, identified MedServ Solutions, Inc. (DUNS: 078451239, CAGE: 8JKP2), located at 2490 Sidney Street, Suite 300, Pittsburgh, PA 15203, as the only certified HUBZone small business concern in the local area with the requisite OEM certifications and trained personnel to service the specific biomedical equipment models currently installed at VA Pittsburgh Healthcare System.

The anticipated total contract value, including options, is $3,850,000, which falls below the $4.5 million threshold for non-manufacturing NAICS codes as required by FAR 19.1306(a)(2). The applicable NAICS code is 811210 – Electronic and Precision Equipment Repair and Maintenance, with a size standard of $34.0 million.

MedServ Solutions, Inc. has previously performed similar work under Contract No. 36C24420C0087 and received satisfactory to exceptional performance ratings in the Contractor Performance Assessment Reporting System (CPARS). The firm has been determined to be a responsible contractor. The requirement is not currently being performed by an 8(a) participant under FAR Subpart 19.8.

SECTION L – INSTRUCTIONS, CONDITIONS, AND NOTICES TO OFFERORS

L.1 GENERAL INFORMATION

Solicitation No.: 70RCSA25R00000081
Agency: Department of Homeland Security, Cybersecurity and Infrastructure Security Agency (CISA)
Title: Cybersecurity Assessment and Vulnerability Scanning Support Services
NAICS Code: 541512 – Computer Systems Design Services (Size Standard: $34.0 million)
Set-Aside: Total HUBZone Small Business Set-Aside
Contract Type: Time-and-Materials with Not-to-Exceed Ceiling

L.2 ELIGIBILITY REQUIREMENTS

This procurement is a total HUBZone set-aside in accordance with FAR 19.1305. Proposals will only be accepted from concerns that are certified by the SBA as HUBZone small business concerns at the time of proposal submission, at the time of award, and throughout the duration of contract performance. Offerors shall include in their proposals a copy of their current HUBZone certification letter issued by the SBA or a screenshot from the SBA's HUBZone certification database confirming active status.

The clause at FAR 52.219-3, Notice of HUBZone Set-Aside or Sole-Source Award, applies to this solicitation. Additionally, FAR 52.219-14, Limitations on Subcontracting, requires that at least 50 percent of the cost of contract performance incurred for personnel be expended for employees of the concern. The HUBZone price evaluation preference described at FAR 52.219-4 shall apply in the event a HUBZone concern's offer is evaluated against a non-HUBZone concern, should the set-aside be partially withdrawn.

L.3 PERIOD OF PERFORMANCE

The anticipated period of performance is a 12-month base period commencing on or about September 1, 2025, with three 12-month option periods and a six-month extension option pursuant to FAR 52.217-8. The estimated total contract value across all periods is $8,500,000.

SOURCES SOUGHT NOTICE / REQUEST FOR INFORMATION
Solicitation Number: 1333ND25SNB340019
Agency: Department of Commerce, National Oceanic and Atmospheric Administration (NOAA), National Weather Service
Subject: Marine Observation Buoy Refurbishment and Deployment Services

The National Weather Service (NWS) is conducting market research to determine the availability of qualified HUBZone certified small business concerns capable of performing refurbishment, calibration, and at-sea deployment of marine meteorological observation buoys along the Gulf Coast region. This sources sought notice is issued in contemplation of a HUBZone small business set-aside under FAR Subpart 19.13.

The anticipated requirement includes the overhaul and sensor recalibration of approximately 35 Coastal-Marine Automated Network (C-MAN) stations and 12 moored buoy platforms annually, including hull cleaning, anti-fouling treatment, battery replacement, data logger programming, and vessel-based deployment. Work will be performed at a contractor-furnished facility within 100 miles of the Gulf Coast and at sea locations as directed by task order.

The applicable NAICS code is 336611 – Ship Building and Repairing, with a small business size standard of 1,300 employees. The estimated contract value is $6,200,000 over a base year and three option years.

Responses to this notice should include: (1) company name, address, CAGE code, and UEI number; (2) confirmation of active HUBZone certification with SBA certification number; (3) a brief capability statement not to exceed five pages; and (4) documentation of past performance on similar marine instrumentation or vessel-based deployment contracts.

If a sufficient number of qualified HUBZone small business concerns respond and the contracting officer determines there is a reasonable expectation that at least two HUBZone concerns will submit competitive proposals at fair market prices, NOAA intends to issue the formal solicitation as a total HUBZone set-aside with the FAR 52.219-3 clause and applicable HUBZone price evaluation preference under FAR 52.219-4. Interested firms must respond no later than June 20, 2025, at 2:00 PM Eastern Time.
hubzone_set_aside

SECTION B – SUPPLIES OR SERVICES AND PRICES

B.1 GENERAL

This solicitation is issued as a HUBZone Small Business Set-Aside in accordance with FAR 19.1305 and FAR 19.1307. Only concerns that are certified by the Small Business Administration (SBA) as HUBZone small business concerns are eligible to submit offers. The clause at FAR 52.219-13, Notice of Set-Aside of Orders, applies to this acquisition. Offerors must be a certified HUBZone small business concern at the time of offer submission and must remain certified through the date of contract award.

The applicable NAICS code for this procurement is 561210 – Facilities Support Services, with a small business size standard of $47.0 million in average annual receipts. The Product Service Code is S216, Housekeeping – Facilities Operations Support.

The U.S. General Services Administration (GSA), Public Buildings Service, Region 4, intends to award a single firm-fixed-price contract for comprehensive facilities maintenance and operations support services at the James H. Quillen Federal Building, located in Kingsport, Tennessee. The period of performance shall consist of one (1) base year and four (4) one-year option periods.

B.2 PRICE EVALUATION PREFERENCE

In accordance with FAR 19.1307, offers from HUBZone small business concerns will receive a price evaluation preference. When the lowest responsive offer is from a large business, the contracting officer shall apply a price evaluation factor of 10 percent to offers from non-HUBZone concerns for evaluation purposes only. This preference shall not apply to the actual contract award price.

B.3 ELIGIBILITY VERIFICATION

Prior to award, the Contracting Officer will verify the apparent successful offeror's HUBZone certification status through the SBA's Dynamic Small Business Search (DSBS) database and SAM.gov. Failure to maintain active HUBZone certification may result in the offeror being deemed ineligible for award.

SOLICITATION NO. 75H71025R00038
U.S. DEPARTMENT OF HEALTH AND HUMAN SERVICES
HEALTH RESOURCES AND SERVICES ADMINISTRATION (HRSA)

SECTION L – INSTRUCTIONS TO OFFERORS

L.1 SET-ASIDE DESIGNATION

This requirement is being procured as a total HUBZone set-aside pursuant to FAR Subpart 19.13 and FAR 19.1305(a). The clause at FAR 52.219-3, Notice of HUBZone Set-Aside or Sole-Source Award, is incorporated by reference. Only offerors that are SBA-certified HUBZone small business concerns under the applicable NAICS code 541611 – Administrative Management and General Management Consulting Services (size standard $24.5M) are eligible to submit proposals.

L.2 MARKET RESEARCH JUSTIFICATION

Market research conducted between September 2024 and January 2025 confirmed that there is a reasonable expectation that at least two responsible HUBZone small business concerns will submit offers at fair market prices for the required program evaluation and technical assistance services in support of HRSA's Bureau of Primary Health Care. Responses to the Sources Sought Notice (No. 75H71025SNR0012) posted on SAM.gov on September 16, 2024, yielded capability statements from seven (7) SBA-certified HUBZone firms demonstrating relevant experience.

L.3 PROPOSAL SUBMISSION REQUIREMENTS

Offerors shall submit proposals in two (2) separate volumes:
  Volume I – Technical Proposal (limited to 50 pages)
  Volume II – Price Proposal (no page limit)

Each offeror must include documentation of current HUBZone certification issued by the SBA, including the firm's certification date, certification number, and principal office address located within a designated HUBZone. Offerors must also certify that at least 35% of their employees reside in a HUBZone as required by 13 CFR 126.200.

Q7: Can a joint venture between a HUBZone firm and a non-HUBZone firm submit a proposal?
A7: Yes, provided the joint venture meets the requirements of 13 CFR 126.616. The HUBZone small business concern must be the managing venturer and perform at least 40% of the work. The joint venture must be SBA-certified as a HUBZone joint venture prior to proposal submission.

Q8: Is subcontracting to non-HUBZone firms permitted?
A8: Yes, however the HUBZone prime contractor must comply with the limitations on subcontracting requirements at FAR 52.219-14 and perform at least 50% of the cost of contract performance incurred for personnel with its own employees for this service contract.

Department of Veterans Affairs
Network Contracting Office (NCO) 19 – Rocky Mountain Region
Solicitation Number: 36C25925Q0187

SUBJECT: Biomedical Equipment Preventive Maintenance and Repair Services – VA Eastern Colorado Health Care System

1.0 OVERVIEW

This is a combined synopsis/solicitation for commercial services prepared in accordance with FAR Subpart 12.6 and FAR 13.5, as supplemented with additional information included in this notice. This announcement constitutes the only solicitation; proposals are being requested and a separate written solicitation will not be issued.

2.0 HUBZone SET-ASIDE

This procurement is set aside exclusively for HUBZone small business concerns in accordance with FAR 19.1305 and Veterans Affairs Acquisition Regulation (VAAR) 819.1305. The clause at FAR 52.219-3, Notice of HUBZone Set-Aside or Sole-Source Award, applies to this acquisition. Only SBA-certified HUBZone small business concerns may submit quotations.

The applicable NAICS code is 811210 – Electronic and Precision Equipment Repair and Maintenance, with a small business size standard of $34.0 million.

3.0 EVALUATION METHODOLOGY

Award will be made to the responsible offeror whose quotation, conforming to the solicitation requirements, represents the best value to the Government considering the following factors: (1) Technical Capability, (2) Past Performance, and (3) Price. Technical Capability and Past Performance, when combined, are approximately equal to Price.

In addition, in accordance with FAR 19.1307 and the HUBZone price evaluation preference, offers from HUBZone small business concerns will be evaluated with a 10 percent price preference applied against non-HUBZone offers, should any such offers be considered in the competitive range.

4.0 PERIOD OF PERFORMANCE
Base Year: July 1, 2025 – June 30, 2026
Option Year 1: July 1, 2026 – June 30, 2027
Option Year 2: July 1, 2027 – June 30, 2028

National Oceanic and Atmospheric Administration (NOAA)
National Weather Service – Office of Observations
Notice Number: 1305-AQ-25-00091-SS

The National Weather Service (NWS) is conducting market research to determine the availability of qualified HUBZone small business concerns capable of providing calibration, maintenance, and logistics support services for the Automated Surface Observing System (ASOS) network across the continental United States and U.S. territories.

Based on the results of this sources sought notice, NOAA intends to issue a solicitation as a HUBZone small business set-aside under FAR Subpart 19.13 if a sufficient number of capable HUBZone concerns are identified. Offerors must be registered in the System for Award Management (SAM) at www.sam.gov and must have a current WOSB certification through the Small Business Administration (SBA) or an SBA-approved third-party certifier at the time of offer submission and through the period of performance. The Contracting Officer will verify WOSB eligibility through the SBA's certification database prior to award.

Offerors are advised that pursuant to FAR 52.219-29, to be eligible for award, the offeror must be a Women-Owned Small Business concern as defined in FAR 2.101 and must comply with the applicable WOSB Program requirements set forth in 13 CFR Part 127. Failure to maintain WOSB status throughout the evaluation and award process may result in the offeror being deemed ineligible. Questions regarding eligibility should be directed to the Contracting Officer identified in Block 7 of Standard Form 1449.

Department of Veterans Affairs
Network Contracting Office 19 – Rocky Mountain Region
RFQ Number: 36C25925Q0482

SUBJECT: Biomedical Equipment Preventive Maintenance and Repair Services – VA Eastern Colorado Health Care System

SET-ASIDE DETERMINATION: This solicitation is issued as a Women-Owned Small Business (WOSB) set-aside in accordance with FAR Subpart 19.15 and the Women-Owned Small Business Federal Contract Program. Only offers from WOSB concerns that meet the eligibility requirements at 13 CFR Part 127 will be considered for award.

NAICS Code: 811210 – Electronic and Precision Equipment Repair and Maintenance
Size Standard: $34.0 million

APPLICABLE CLAUSES AND PROVISIONS:
The following FAR clauses and provisions are incorporated into this solicitation by reference pursuant to FAR 52.252-2:

52.219-29 Notice of Set-Aside for, or Sole-Source Award to, Women-Owned Small Business Concerns Eligible Under the Women-Owned Small Business Program (Mar 2023)
52.219-14 Limitations on Subcontracting (Oct 2022)
52.219-1 Small Business Program Representations (Mar 2023)
52.222-3 Convict Labor (Jun 2003)
52.222-50 Combating Trafficking in Persons (Nov 2021)

ELIGIBILITY: Quoters must represent that they are a WOSB concern eligible under the WOSB Program. Quoters must be certified as a WOSB through the SBA's certification process. Quoters that do not meet the definition of a Women-Owned Small Business concern as defined under FAR 2.101 will not be considered for award under this solicitation.

U.S. Army Corps of Engineers – Savannah District
Solicitation No.: W912HN-25-R-0063

Environmental Remediation Support Services – Fort Stewart, Georgia

This announcement constitutes the only solicitation; proposals are being requested and a written solicitation will not be issued separately. This solicitation is issued as a Request for Proposals (RFP) in accordance with FAR Part 15, Contracting by Negotiation.

SET-ASIDE STATUS: This acquisition is 100% set aside for Women-Owned Small Business (WOSB) concerns under the authority of FAR 19.1505 and the Women-Owned Small Business Program.

NAICS Code: 562910 – Remediation Services
Small Business Size Standard: $25.0 million (average annual receipts)

To be eligible for award under this WOSB set-aside, offerors must meet all of the following criteria: (1) the concern must be at least 51 percent unconditionally and directly owned and controlled by one or more women who are United States citizens; (2) the concern must be small under the applicable NAICS code; (3) the concern must be certified as a WOSB by the U.S. Small Business Administration; and (4) the concern must perform at least 50 percent of the cost of the contract incurred for personnel with its own employees, per FAR 52.219-14, Limitations on Subcontracting.

The following provisions and clauses apply to this acquisition:
FAR 52.219-29 – Notice of Set-Aside for, or Sole-Source Award to, Women-Owned Small Business Concerns Eligible Under the Women-Owned Small Business Program (Mar 2023)
FAR 52.219-14 – Limitations on Subcontracting (Oct 2022)
DFARS 252.219-7003 – Small Business Subcontracting Plan (DoD Contracts) (Dec 2019)

Offerors shall submit proof of current WOSB certification with their proposal. The Government reserves the right to verify WOSB status at any point during the procurement process.

AMENDMENT OF SOLICITATION/MODIFICATION OF CONTRACT
U.S. Department of Agriculture – Agricultural Research Service
Solicitation No.: AG-6395-S-25-0021
Amendment No.: 0003

Purpose of Amendment: This amendment is issued to clarify the set-aside determination and to incorporate additional questions and answers from the pre-proposal conference held on March 12, 2025.

1. SET-ASIDE CLARIFICATION:

The Contracting Officer confirms that this acquisition for Laboratory Information Management System (LIMS) Software Development and Support Services is set aside for Women-Owned Small Business (WOSB) concerns under the WOSB Program, as authorized by FAR 19.1505. This is NOT an Economically Disadvantaged Women-Owned Small Business (EDWOSB) set-aside. The solicitation is restricted to WOSB-certified firms only.

NAICS: 541511 – Custom Computer Programming Services
Size Standard: $34.0 million

Offerors must hold a current, valid WOSB certification issued by the SBA at the time of offer and at the time of award. The firm must be unconditionally owned and controlled by one or more women who are U.S. citizens, and must be small under the assigned NAICS code. The clause at FAR 52.219-29, Notice of Set-Aside for, or Sole-Source Award to, Women-Owned Small Business Concerns Eligible Under the Women-Owned Small Business Program, is applicable and is hereby incorporated by reference.

2. QUESTIONS AND ANSWERS:

Q7: Can a joint venture between a WOSB and a large business submit a proposal?
A7: No. Only firms that are themselves certified WOSB concerns under the SBA's WOSB Program are eligible. Joint ventures must independently qualify as WOSB concerns and meet the requirements of 13 CFR 127.506.

Q8: Is past performance under prior EDWOSB contracts acceptable as relevant experience?
A8: Yes, past performance references from EDWOSB or WOSB contracts are acceptable as relevant experience for evaluation under Factor 2.

All other terms and conditions remain unchanged.

Solicitation Number: W911NF-25-R-0188
Agency: U.S. Army Contracting Command – Aberdeen Proving Ground
Office: ACC-APG, Research Triangle Park Division
4300 S. Miami Blvd, Suite 300, Durham, NC 27703

Set-Aside Determination: Women-Owned Small Business (WOSB) Program Set-Aside

Pursuant to FAR Subpart 19.15 and FAR 52.219-29, this solicitation is issued as a total Women-Owned Small Business (WOSB) set-aside. The Contracting Officer has determined, based on market research conducted between 12 January 2025 and 04 February 2025, that there is a reasonable expectation that two or more responsible WOSB concerns will submit offers at fair market prices for the services described herein.

NAICS Code: 561210 – Facilities Support Services
Small Business Size Standard: $47,000,000.00

Description of Services: The contractor shall provide comprehensive facilities management, operations, and maintenance support services at multiple U.S. Army Research Laboratory installations in the Research Triangle Park area. Services include preventive and corrective maintenance of HVAC systems, electrical distribution, fire suppression systems, plumbing, building automation systems, grounds maintenance, and custodial services. The contractor shall maintain a qualified on-site workforce and provide 24/7 emergency response capability.

Contract Type: Indefinite Delivery/Indefinite Quantity (IDIQ), Time-and-Materials
Ordering Period: Five (5) years
Minimum Guarantee: $50,000.00
Maximum Ceiling: $18,500,000.00

Offerors must be certified as a WOSB concern in SAM.gov at the time of initial offer and must maintain such certification through the duration of any resulting contract. Joint ventures must comply with 13 CFR 127.506.

SOURCES SOUGHT / MARKET RESEARCH SYNOPSIS

Agency: U.S. Department of Veterans Affairs
Facility: VA North Texas Health Care System
Notice Number: 36C25725S0093
Posted Date: March 18, 2025
Response Deadline: April 01, 2025, 2:00 PM CDT

The Department of Veterans Affairs, Network Contracting Office 17 (NCO 17), is conducting market research to identify potential Women-Owned Small Business (WOSB) concerns capable of providing Medical Transcription and Clinical Documentation Improvement (CDI) Services for the VA North Texas Health Care System, Dallas, TX and its associated Community Based Outpatient Clinics (CBOCs).

NAICS Code: 561410 – Document Preparation Services
Product/Service Code: Q999
Size Standard: $14,000,000

Based on preliminary market research, this procurement is anticipated to be set aside exclusively for Women-Owned Small Business (WOSB) concerns under the WOSB Program in accordance with FAR 19.1505(b) and the applicable provisions of FAR 52.219-29. The VA has identified this NAICS code as one designated by the Small Business Administration for WOSB contracting. The Contracting Officer seeks to validate that sufficient WOSB competition exists in this service area.

Interested WOSB firms are requested to submit a capability statement (no more than 10 pages) demonstrating relevant past performance, technical qualifications, and staffing resources. Responses must include: (1) firm name, address, DUNS/UEI number, and cage code; (2) current WOSB certification status through SBA's certification portal; (3) at least two references for similar medical transcription/CDI contracts within the last five years; and (4) brief narrative of approach.

Point of Contact: Sandra K. Villanueva, Contract Specialist, sandra.villanueva@va.gov, (214) 857-1033. This is a sources sought notice for informational and planning purposes only and does not constitute a solicitation.

Agency: U.S. Department of the Interior, Bureau of Reclamation
Region: Columbia-Pacific Northwest Region
Address: 1150 N. Curtis Road, Suite 100, Boise, ID 83706

L.1 GENERAL INSTRUCTIONS

This solicitation is issued as a Total Women-Owned Small Business (WOSB) Set-Aside under the authority of FAR Subpart 19.15 and Public Law 111-240. The clause at FAR 52.219-29 applies to this acquisition. Offers received from firms that are not certified WOSBs will not be evaluated and will be rejected as non-responsive.

L.2 ELIGIBILITY REQUIREMENTS

To be eligible for award under this WOSB set-aside, the offeror must: (a) be a small business concern under NAICS 237110 (Water and Sewer Line and Related Structures Construction), size standard $45,000,000; (b) be at least 51 percent unconditionally and directly owned and controlled by one or more women who are United States citizens; (c) have one or more women manage the day-to-day operations and make long-term decisions for the business; and (d) be certified as a WOSB in the SBA's certification program through SAM.gov (or legacy certify.sba.gov portal) at time of offer and through contract performance.

L.3 PROPOSAL SUBMISSION

Offerors shall submit proposals electronically via the Bureau of Reclamation's e-Procurement portal no later than June 12, 2025, 4:00 PM Mountain Time. Proposals shall consist of three separate volumes: Volume I – Technical Approach (50-page limit), Volume II – Past Performance (30-page limit), and Volume III – Price. Each volume shall be submitted as a separate PDF file not exceeding 25 MB.

L.4 SUBCONTRACTING LIMITATIONS

In accordance with FAR 52.219-14, Limitations on Subcontracting, the WOSB prime contractor must perform at least 15 percent of the cost of the contract with its own employees for construction contracts.

Contracting Officer: Jennifer A. Whitfield, jwhitfield@usbr.gov, (208) 378-5312

Set-Aside: This requirement is set aside exclusively for Women-Owned Small Business (WOSB) concerns. A review of the Dynamic Small Business Search, SAM.gov profiles, and GSA Schedule holders identified no fewer than six (6) certified WOSBs with relevant past performance in GIS services for federal civil works or environmental mission areas. Work will be performed primarily at the Fort Worth District offices with occasional travel to project sites across the Southwestern Division area of responsibility.

C.3 VAAR 852.219-73 VA NOTICE OF TOTAL SET-ASIDE FOR CERTIFIED SERVICE-DISABLED VETERAN-OWNED SMALL BUSINESSES (JAN 2023) (DEVIATION)

(a) Definition. for the Department of Veterans Affairs, ''Service-disabled Veteran-owned small business concern or SDVOSB'':
(1) Means a small business concern—
(i) Not less than 51 percent of which is owned by one or more service-disabled Veterans or, in the case of any publicly owned business, not less than 51 percent of the stock of which is owned by one or more service-disabled Veterans or eligible surviving spouses...

(b) General. In order for a concern to submit an offer and be eligible for the award of an SDVOSB set-aside or sole source contract, the concern must qualify as a small business concern under the size standard corresponding to the NAICS code assigned to the contract and be listed as an SDVOSB participant in the SBA certification database as set forth in 13 CFR 128.
(1) Offers received from entities that are not certified SDVOSBs and listed in the SBA certification database at the time of offer shall not be considered.
(2) Any award resulting from this solicitation shall be made to a certified SDVOSB listed in the SBA certification database who is eligible at the time of submission of offer(s) and at the time of award.

C.6 VAAR 852.219-73 VA NOTICE OF TOTAL SET-ASIDE FOR CERTIFIED SERVICE-DISABLED VETERAN-OWNED SMALL BUSINESSES (JAN 2023) (DEVIATION)
(a) Definition. for the Department of Veterans Affairs, ''Service-disabled Veteran-owned small business concern or SDVOSB'':
(1) Means a small business concern—
(i) Not less than 51 percent of which is owned by one or more service-disabled Veterans or, in the case of any publicly owned business, not less than 51 percent of the stock of which is owned by one or more service-disabled Veterans or eligible surviving spouses...
(b) General. In order for a concern to submit an offer and be eligible for the award of an SDVOSB set-aside or sole source contract, the concern must qualify as a small business concern under the size standard corresponding to the NAICS code assigned to the contract and be listed as an SDVOSB participant in the SBA certification database as set forth in 13 CFR 128.
(1) Offers received from entities that are not certified SDVOSBs and listed in the SBA certification database at the time of offer shall not be considered.
(2) Any award resulting from this solicitation shall be made to a certified SDVOSB listed in the SBA certification database who is eligible at the time of submission of offer(s) and at the time of award.

One of the purposes of issuing this RFI is to determine the availability of Small Business Administration (SBA) Veteran Small Business Certification Program (VetCert) Service-Disabled Veteran-Owned Small Businesses (SDVOSBs) and Veteran Owned Small Businesses (VOSBs) that are capable of providing the services identified in the attached Performance Work Statement (PWS) that are not T4NG Contract holders. An SDVOSB/VOSB company that is not in the SBA certification database (https://veterans.certify.sba.gov/) should not respond to this notice.

The North American Industry Classification System (NAICS) for this requirement is 541512 with a size standard of $34 million.

Your company's intent and ability to meet the set aside requirement in accordance with VAAR 852.219-73 (JAN 2023) (DEVIATION) VA Notice of Total Set-Aside For Certified SDVOSBs and 13 CFR §125.6, which states the contractor will not pay more than 50% of the amount paid by the government to the prime for contract performance to firms that are not certified SDVOSBs listed in the SBA certification database (excluding direct costs to the extent they are not the principal purpose of the acquisition and the SDVOSB/VOSB does not provide the service, such as airline travel, cloud computing services, or mass media purchases). When a contract includes both services and supplies, the 50 percent limitation shall apply only to the service portion of the contract.

PRESOLICITATION NOTICE – Initial Outfitting, Transition, and Activation Services in support of The Villages, FL Outpatient Clinic.

100% SDVOSB SET ASIDE

This is a Pre-Solicitation Notice that the Veterans Health Administration (VHA) intends to solicit quotes to provide non-personal Initial Outfitting, Transition, and Activation (IOT&A) services for The Villages, FL Outpatient Clinic (OPC). This requirement supports VHA's mission by providing contractor support services to help bring the hospital into full planned operations that will provide health care services to Veterans. This will be a firm-fixed-price contract with an estimated Period of Performance of 40-months.

This requirement will be set-aside 100% for Service-Disabled Veteran-Owned Small Businesses (SDVOSBs) using FAR Part 12 and 37. To be eligible for an SDVOSB set aside under VA's Veterans First Contracting Program, SDVOSBs must:

be verified by the Department of Veterans Affairs (VA) as SDVOSB (see https://search.certifications.sba.gov/ for details)

and

meet the small business size standard for NAICS 541614.

Area of Interest 1: Air Mobility Command (AMC) A6 – Base Information Technology Modernization – Please note that responses will only be accepted from Service-Disabled Veteran Owned Small Businesses (SDVOSBs)

Introduction & Mission Objective
Air Mobility Command (AMC) seeks innovative partners to lead a comprehensive transformation of its Information Technology (IT) infrastructure. The current legacy environment is outdated, inefficient, and hinders the "Global Reach" mission by compromising operational speed, data sharing, and overall warfighter effectiveness.

The objective is to overhaul this system by assessing existing architecture, developing a forward-looking roadmap, and integrating modern technologies across core, cloud, and edge environments. The goal is to field a secure, scalable, and purpose-built IT infrastructure that enables data-driven, globally maneuverable airpower for modern joint warfare.

For the purposes of this RFI, the government is seeking solutions that primarily live in the mission-level MSP&A analytical area.

Offerors must provide relevant experience examples that demonstrate human capital orchestration using the self-score worksheet. Offerors may provide up to two (2) examples for this element.

A total of 104 contractor-personnel support DevSecOps, service delivery, and infrastructure needs under this task order for secure and efficient case processing and vetting of applicants. As a result of the RFO initiative in support of Executive Order (E.O.) 14275, Restoring Common Sense to Federal Procurement, there were significant changes made to the acquisition strategy, period of performance, total estimated procurement value, and total number of teams needed for ODOS IV. While the Agency continues to finalize its long-term strategy for ODOS IV, it was determined that it was in the Government's best interest to pursue a smaller, competitive version of ODOS (referred to as ODOS 3.1). The succeeding competitive procurement, ODOS 3.1, was expected to be in place prior to the expiration of the current task order but has faced numerous delays in both the solicitation and evaluation phases, consequently prolonging the Government's efforts to transition work from the ODOS III contractors to new awardees. The Government has determined that a non-competitive extension to the current award is the most efficient method to continue system maintenance and the current iteration of ODOS III requirements under the ADG-REI task order until the ensuing awards are made and sufficient time is given to the new awardees to transition in.

1.8 Task 8 – Program Management Support

1.8.0.1 The Contractor shall provide the comprehensive program management, project planning, and personnel management required to ensure the successful and efficient execution of all contract requirements. This includes providing a designated Program Manager as a single point of accountability, developing and maintaining a detailed Project Management Plan (PMP), and managing a qualified workforce with the specific expertise necessary to support the DAF/A10 mission.

Key Personnel positions include: PM, International Treaties SMEs, FCP SME, CWMD SME, CWMD Computer Programming SME, Nuclear Stockpile Stewardship SME, IAMD SME, A10 Portfolio Intelligence SME, and NC3 SME.

Key Personnel Qualifications:
Program Manager: Must have demonstrated experience planning, directing, and managing complex projects of similar size and scope, including experience with DAF/DoW leadership.
International Treaties and Agreements SME: Must have demonstrated knowledge and experience with specific treaties (e.g., New START, CWC, NPT), compliance activities, and reporting systems (e.g., ACES).
Foreign Clearance Program (FCP) SME: Must have demonstrated experience with the DoW FCG, international entry requirements, sovereignty issues, and APACS policy.
CWMD SME: Must possess an active TS/SCI clearance and have extensive experience with CWMD/Counterproliferation programs, CBRN science, operational impacts, and Modeling & Simulation (M&S) tools.
CWMD Computer Programming SME: Must be an expert programmer in Python, C++, and Visual Basic, capable of modeling complex systems involving chemical, biological, radiological, or nuclear effects on air operations.
Nuclear Stockpile Stewardship SME: Must have demonstrated knowledge of nuclear stockpile stewardship at all government levels and experience working with the broader nuclear enterprise.
IAMD SME: Must possess an active TS/SCI clearance and have demonstrated expertise in IAMD strategy, policy, doctrine, technologies, and the SPPBE process.
A10 Portfolio Intelligence SME: Must have demonstrated knowledge and research experience in Nuclear and CWMD-related topics.
Nuclear Command, Control and Communications (NC3) SME: Must have demonstrated experience with NC2 authorities, C2 relationships, and the waveforms, technologies, and platforms associated with the NC3 enterprise weapon system.

SECTION B – SUPPLIES OR SERVICES AND PRICES/COSTS

B.1 The total not-to-exceed (NTE) amount for this firm-fixed-price contract is $47,500.00, inclusive of all labor, materials, travel, and incidental costs for the full period of performance.

B.2 DESCRIPTION OF SERVICES

The U.S. Fish and Wildlife Service (USFWS), Region 3 – Great Lakes/Big Rivers, requires the services of a qualified contractor to conduct a comprehensive wetland delineation and habitat assessment at the Neal Smith National Wildlife Refuge in Prairie City, Iowa. The assessment shall cover approximately 1,200 acres of restored tallgrass prairie and associated wetland complexes.

B.3 SCOPE OF WORK

The contractor shall perform the following tasks:
(a) Conduct jurisdictional wetland delineations in accordance with the 1987 U.S. Army Corps of Engineers Wetland Delineation Manual and the Midwest Regional Supplement.
(b) Prepare detailed mapping products using GIS-compatible formats (shapefile and geodatabase).
(c) Produce a final technical report documenting all delineation findings, vegetation community classifications, and recommended management actions.
(d) Deliver one (1) draft report for government review within 60 calendar days of the notice to proceed, and one (1) final report within 30 calendar days of receipt of government comments.

B.4 PERIOD OF PERFORMANCE

The base period of performance shall be six (6) months from the date of contract award, with no option periods anticipated.

B.5 INVOICING

The contractor shall submit invoices monthly via the Invoice Processing Platform (IPP) in accordance with FAR 52.232-33, Payment by Electronic Funds Transfer—System for Award Management. All invoices shall reference the contract number and applicable CLIN.

REQUEST FOR PROPOSALS
Solicitation Number: USCG-SML-26-R-0019
United States Coast Guard, Shore Maintenance Logistics Command
Base Cleveland, Ohio

PART I – OVERVIEW AND SCHEDULE

1.1 The United States Coast Guard (USCG) is seeking proposals from qualified small businesses for the repair and refurbishment of two (2) aluminum gangway structures located at USCG Station Cleveland Harbor, Cleveland, Ohio. This requirement is set aside 100% for small business concerns under FAR 19.502-2.

1.2 The total contract budget for this requirement is $63,400.00, representing the maximum contract value inclusive of all labor, materials, equipment, transportation, surface preparation, protective coatings, and any applicable warranties. The Government will award a single firm-fixed-price contract.

1.3 PERIOD OF PERFORMANCE
Work shall commence no later than fifteen (15) calendar days after contract award and shall be completed within sixty (60) calendar days of commencement.

1.4 BACKGROUND
The two gangway structures (designated GW-04 and GW-07) provide personnel access between the station pier and moored 87-foot Marine Protector Class cutters. Both gangways have sustained corrosion damage, structural fatigue cracking at weld joints, and degradation of non-skid walking surfaces. A structural condition survey conducted by USCG Civil Engineering Unit Cleveland in October 2025 rated both gangways as "C-3 – Poor" under the USCG Shore Facility Capital Asset Management (SFCAM) framework.

1.5 INSPECTION
A mandatory site visit is scheduled for March 18, 2026, at 10:00 AM local time. Prospective offerors must RSVP to the Contracting Specialist no later than March 14, 2026. Proposals from firms that do not attend the site visit will not be considered.

1.6 PROPOSAL DUE DATE
Proposals are due no later than April 4, 2026, at 4:00 PM EST, submitted electronically through SAM.gov.

SECTION 1 – GENERAL INFORMATION

Procurement Title: Annual Fire Extinguisher Inspection and Maintenance Services
Solicitation No.: SPE8E8-25-T-0043
Issuing Activity: Defense Logistics Agency (DLA) Installation Management – Fort Gregg-Adams
Contract Type: Firm-Fixed-Price
Total Contract Value: $8,950.00

SECTION 2 – SCOPE
The Defense Logistics Agency requires a licensed fire protection contractor to perform annual inspection, testing, maintenance, and recharging (as needed) of approximately 385 portable fire extinguishers and 12 pre-engineered kitchen hood suppression systems located across Buildings 2502 through 2519 at DLA Installation Support, Fort Gregg-Adams, Virginia.

All work shall be performed in accordance with NFPA 10 (Standard for Portable Fire Extinguishers), NFPA 17A (Standard for Wet Chemical Extinguishing Systems), and the manufacturer's maintenance guidelines. The Contractor shall provide all labor, materials, replacement parts, extinguishing agents, and transportation required to complete the inspections.

SECTION 3 – PERIOD OF PERFORMANCE
Base Year: 1 October 2025 through 30 September 2026. No option periods are contemplated.

SECTION 4 – INSPECTION AND ACCEPTANCE
The COR shall inspect completed work within five (5) business days of the Contractor's notification of completion. Acceptance or rejection will be documented in writing. Deficient items shall be corrected at no additional cost to the Government within ten (10) business days of notification.

SECTION 5 – INVOICING

JUSTIFICATION FOR OTHER THAN FULL AND OPEN COMPETITION

1. CONTRACTING ACTIVITY
National Oceanic and Atmospheric Administration (NOAA), National Weather Service, Eastern Region Headquarters, Bohemia, NY

2. NATURE AND/OR DESCRIPTION OF THE ACTION
The contracting activity intends to award a sole-source purchase order to DataStream Analytics, LLC for the maintenance and calibration of three (3) Automated Surface Observing System (ASOS) peripheral sensor arrays located at weather forecast offices within the Eastern Region. This procurement includes on-site calibration services, diagnostic testing, replacement of worn sensor components, and delivery of a comprehensive calibration verification report for each site.

3. ESTIMATED VALUE
The total contract value is estimated at $47,800.00.

4. AUTHORITY CITED
FAR 6.302-1 – Only one responsible source and no other supplies or services will satisfy agency requirements.

5. REASON FOR AUTHORITY CITED
DataStream Analytics, LLC is the original equipment manufacturer's sole authorized service provider for ASOS peripheral sensor calibration within the Continental United States. Use of an unauthorized service provider would void existing manufacturer warranties and could compromise data quality essential to public weather forecasting. No other vendor possesses the proprietary firmware tools and calibration standards required to service these units.

6. MARKET RESEARCH
The Contracting Officer conducted market research via SAM.gov and contacted three potential alternative vendors. None possessed the required OEM authorization or proprietary calibration tools. Results confirm that DataStream Analytics, LLC remains the only responsible source.

7. ACTIONS TO REMOVE BARRIERS TO COMPETITION
The agency will continue to evaluate alternative service arrangements during subsequent procurement cycles.

2.1 PURPOSE
The City of Cedar Falls, Iowa, Department of Public Works seeks qualified contractors to perform a comprehensive condition assessment and remaining useful life analysis of the municipally owned pedestrian bridge spanning the Cedar River at Main Street. The structure, originally constructed in 1987, has not undergone a full engineering assessment since 2011.

2.2 CONTRACT VALUE AND TYPE
This procurement will result in a single firm-fixed-price contract. The total contract value shall not exceed $37,000.00. This amount represents the maximum the City will obligate and includes all professional engineering services, field inspection, materials testing, structural modeling, report preparation, and any associated travel or equipment costs. Field inspection activities are expected to occur between August 1 and September 15, 2026, when river levels are historically at their lowest. A draft assessment report shall be submitted within forty-five (45) calendar days of the completion of field work, and the final report shall be delivered no later than thirty (30) days after receipt of City comments on the draft.

2.4 MINIMUM QUALIFICATIONS
The lead structural engineer assigned to this project must be a licensed Professional Engineer (P.E.) in the State of Iowa or capable of obtaining reciprocal licensure prior to commencement of field work. The firm must demonstrate at least three (3) completed bridge condition assessments for municipal or state clients within the past five (5) years. References shall be provided with the technical proposal.

Product/Service Code: R499

1.0 DESCRIPTION
The Indian Health Service (IHS), Phoenix Area Office, seeks a qualified small business vendor to develop and deliver a series of culturally responsive health literacy training modules for use across six tribal health facilities in central Arizona. The modules shall address chronic disease self-management, diabetes prevention, and behavioral health awareness, and must be developed in collaboration with tribal community health representatives.

2.0 TOTAL CONTRACT VALUE
The total obligated amount for this contract action is $62,000.00. This is a single-award, firm-fixed-price purchase order. The $62,000.00 represents the complete funding for all tasks described herein, including development, pilot testing, revision, and final delivery of all training materials in both digital and print-ready formats.

3.0 EVALUATION CRITERIA
Award will be made on a best-value basis considering the following factors in descending order of importance: (1) Technical Approach and Cultural Competency, (2) Past Performance on similar engagements with tribal or underserved populations, and (3) Price. The Government intends to evaluate quotations and make award without discussions; therefore, offerors are encouraged to submit their best terms initially.

4.0 PERIOD OF PERFORMANCE
The period of performance is six (6) months from date of award. All deliverables must be accepted by the Contracting Officer's Representative (COR) prior to final payment.

Section L.5.3 – Staffing Plan Submission Instructions

Offerors shall submit a detailed Staffing Plan as Volume III of their proposal in response to Solicitation No. 75D30125R00048, issued by the Centers for Disease Control and Prevention (CDC), Center for Surveillance, Epidemiology, and Laboratory Services (CSELS). The Staffing Plan shall demonstrate the Offeror's ability to provide a minimum of 14 FTEs to support the Public Health Data Modernization Initiative (DMI) across the base year and four option years.

The plan must address recruitment, retention, and transition strategies for the following labor categories, at minimum:

- Project Manager (1 FTE)
- Senior Data Engineer (2 FTEs)
- Data Engineer – Mid-Level (2 FTEs)
- Cloud Solutions Architect (1 FTE)
- Senior Epidemiological Informaticist (1 FTE)
- FHIR/HL7 Interoperability Specialist (1 FTE)
- Senior Full-Stack Developer (2 FTEs)
- Front-End Developer (1 FTE)
- Data Visualization Analyst (1 FTE)
- Cybersecurity/ATO Specialist (1 FTE)
- DevOps/CI-CD Pipeline Engineer (1 FTE)

Total proposed staffing levels shall not fall below 14 FTEs without prior written approval from the Contracting Officer. Offerors should reference the labor category descriptions in Attachment J-3 and map each proposed individual (or notional candidate) to the qualification requirements therein. The evaluation criteria in Section M.3 assign significant weight to the realism and feasibility of the proposed staffing approach. Proposals that do not clearly demonstrate the Offeror's capacity to sustain the required headcount throughout the period of performance may be assessed as having a significant weakness. This requirement is issued under FAR Part 16.5 as a cost-plus-fixed-fee task order.

3.2 Staffing Requirements

The Contractor shall provide a minimum of 15 Full-Time Equivalent (FTE) personnel to support the Department of Veterans Affairs (VA) Office of Information and Technology (OIT) Enterprise Cloud Modernization Program. The Contractor's staffing plan shall include, at a minimum, the following named positions:

1. Program Manager (Key Personnel)
2. Deputy Program Manager
3. Enterprise Cloud Architect (Key Personnel)
4. Senior DevSecOps Engineer
5. DevSecOps Engineer (2 positions)
6. Cloud Security Engineer (Key Personnel)
7. Senior Systems Engineer
8. Systems Engineer
9. Database Migration Specialist
10. Site Reliability Engineer (2 positions)
11. Configuration Management Specialist
12. Quality Assurance Lead
13. Technical Writer/Documentation Specialist
14. Help Desk/Tier II Support Specialist

All personnel shall possess, at a minimum, an active Public Trust (Tier 2) background investigation or higher. The Cloud Security Engineer and Senior DevSecOps Engineer shall hold current certifications in at least one FedRAMP-authorized cloud platform (e.g., AWS GovCloud, Azure Government, or Google Cloud for Government). The Program Manager shall have a minimum of ten (10) years of progressive IT program management experience, including at least five (5) years managing cloud migration or modernization efforts within a Federal civilian agency context. The Contractor shall ensure continuity of staffing and shall not reduce staffing levels below the 15 FTE minimum without prior written approval from the Contracting Officer's Representative (COR). In accordance with VAAR 852.237-70, the Contractor shall notify the COR within five (5) business days of any personnel departures and shall provide qualified replacement personnel within thirty (30) calendar days. Staffing plans shall be reviewed quarterly as part of the Contract Performance Assessment Report (CPAR) cycle. The Government reserves the right to approve or reject proposed substitutions for Key Personnel positions identified above.

PWS 5.0 PERSONNEL REQUIREMENTS

5.1 General

The Contractor shall provide qualified personnel to support the Defense Logistics Agency (DLA) J6 Enterprise Technology Services Directorate in the operation, maintenance, and modernization of enterprise IT systems supporting global logistics operations. The total estimated effort for this contract is 104 contractor personnel across DLA Headquarters (Fort Belvoir, VA), DLA Distribution Centers, and DLA Information Operations (Columbus, OH).

5.2 Labor Category Summary

The following table summarizes the minimum staffing by labor category:

Program Manager – 1
Deputy Program Manager – 1
Senior Network Engineer – 6
Network Engineer – 10
Senior Systems Administrator (Windows) – 8
Senior Systems Administrator (Linux/UNIX) – 6
Database Administrator (Oracle) – 5
Database Administrator (SQL Server) – 4
Cybersecurity Analyst (IAT Level III) – 8
Cybersecurity Engineer – 4
Service Desk Manager – 1
Service Desk Analyst (Tier I) – 14
Service Desk Analyst (Tier II) – 10
Enterprise Monitoring Specialist – 6
Storage/Backup Engineer – 4
Virtualization Engineer – 4
IT Asset Management Specialist – 3
Configuration Management Analyst – 3
Quality Assurance Specialist – 2
Technical Writer – 2
Training Specialist – 2

5.3 All Cybersecurity Analysts and Cybersecurity Engineers shall hold current DoD 8570.01-M compliant certifications at the applicable IA Technical (IAT) or IA Management (IAM) levels. Personnel performing work on DLA networks shall obtain and maintain a favorable National Agency Check with Local Agency Check and Credit Check (NACLC) or equivalent Tier 3 investigation. In accordance with DFARS 252.204-7012, all contractor personnel shall complete Cyber Awareness training within 30 days of contract start. The Contractor shall submit monthly staffing reports to the COR identifying each filled and vacant position, hire dates, and projected fill dates for any vacancies exceeding 15 business days.

Section 8(a) Award (Oct 2019)
(a) By execution of a contract, the Small Business Administration (SBA) agrees to the following:
(1) To furnish the supplies or services set forth in the contract according to the specifications and the terms and conditions by subcontracting with the Offeror who has been determined an eligible concern pursuant to the provisions of section 8(a) of the Small Business Act, as amended (15 U.S.C.637(a)).
(2) Except for novation agreements, delegates to the 127 MSC the responsibility for administering the contract with complete authority to take any action on behalf of the Government under the terms and conditions of the contract; provided, however that the contracting agency shall give advance notice to the SBA before it issues a final notice terminating the right of the subcontractor to proceed with further performance, either in whole or in part, under the contract.
(3) That payments to be made under the contract will be made directly to the subcontractor by the contracting activity.

52.219-18 Notification of Competition Limited to Eligible 8(a) Participants.
Notification of Competition Limited to Eligible 8(a) Participants (Oct 2022)
(a) Offers are solicited only from—
(1) Small business concerns expressly certified by the Small Business Administration (SBA) for participation in SBA's 8(a) program and which meet the following criteria at the time of submission of offer—
(i) The Offeror is in conformance with the 8(a) support limitation set forth in its approved business plan; and
(ii) The Offeror is in conformance with the Business Activity Targets set forth in its approved business plan or any remedial action directed by SBA;
(2) A joint venture, in which at least one of the 8(a) program participants that is a party to the joint venture complies with the criteria set forth in paragraph (a)(1) of this clause, that complies with 13 CFR 124.513(c); or
(3) A joint venture—
(i) That is comprised of a mentor and an 8(a) protégé with an approved mentor-protégé agreement under the 8(a) program;
(ii) In which at least one of the 8(a) program participants that is a party to the joint venture complies with the criteria set forth in paragraph (a)(1) of this clause; and
(iii) That complies with 13 CFR 124.513(c).
(b) By submission of its offer, the Offeror represents that it meets the applicable criteria set forth in paragraph (a) of this clause.
(c) Any award resulting from this solicitation will be made to the Small Business Administration, which will subcontract performance to the successful 8(a) offeror selected through the evaluation criteria set forth in this solicitation.

252.219-7009 Section 8(a) Direct Award.
SECTION 8(a) DIRECT AWARD (OCT 2018)
(a) This contract is issued as a direct award between the contracting office and the 8(a) Contractor pursuant to the Partnership Agreement between the Small Business Administration (SBA) and the Department of Defense. Accordingly, the SBA, even if not identified in Section A of this contract, is the prime contractor and retains responsibility for 8(a) certification, for 8(a) eligibility determinations and related issues, and for providing counseling and assistance to the 8(a) Contractor under the 8(a) Program.
(b) The contracting office is responsible for administering the contract and for taking any action on behalf of the Government under the terms and conditions of the contract; provided that the contracting office shall give advance notice to the SBA before it issues a final notice terminating performance, either in whole or in part, under the contract. The contracting office also shall coordinate with the SBA prior to processing any novation agreement. The contracting office may assign contract administration functions to a contract administration office.
(c) The 8(a) Contractor agrees that it will notify the Contracting Officer, simultaneous with its notification to the SBA (as required by SBA's 8(a) regulations at 13 CFR 124.515), when the owner or owners upon whom 8(a) eligibility is based plan to relinquish ownership or control of the concern.

Award shall be made to the 8(a) offeror whose proposal offers the best value based upon the evaluation criteria outlines in this RFP. By submission of its proposal, the offeror accedes to all RFP requirements. If five (5) or fewer proposals are received, the Government shall evaluate all proposals for technical acceptability. The Department of Veterans Affairs, Network Contracting Office 4, is conducting market research to identify qualified 8(a) certified small business concerns capable of providing comprehensive janitorial, custodial, and infection control cleaning services at the Coatesville VA Medical Center, Coatesville, PA 19320.

The previous contract (36C244-20-D-0083) was awarded as an 8(a) sole source action to ProClean Services, Inc. (SBA-certified 8(a) participant), with a period of performance ending June 30, 2025. The VA is now evaluating whether this follow-on requirement should be procured as an 8(a) competitive set-aside, 8(a) sole source under FAR 19.805-1(a)(2), or an unrestricted full and open competition.

In order to determine whether an 8(a) set-aside is appropriate, the Government requires responses from at least two (2) 8(a)-certified small businesses demonstrating the requisite experience, capability, and capacity to perform these services. Respondents must clearly identify their SBA 8(a) certification status, 8(a) program entry and graduation dates, and provide evidence of relevant past performance on contracts of similar size, scope, and complexity.

All interested 8(a) firms shall submit a capability statement not to exceed ten (10) pages, including: (1) company profile and 8(a) certification documentation; (2) evidence of compliance with FAR 52.219-14 Limitations on Subcontracting; (3) at least two (2) past performance references for custodial services in healthcare environments; and (4) DUNS/UEI number and SAM.gov registration verification.

This notice is for market research and planning purposes only. It does not constitute a solicitation and shall not be construed as a commitment by the Government to issue a solicitation or award a contract.

Assumptions
1. Software Vendor: CMS intends to procure a SaaS based COTS platform, through a competitive process, to be configured and extended by the vendor in collaboration with CMS, MACs, and/or supporting contractors.
2. Professional Services: CMS envisions its primary role as policy development and oversight and expects to contract for operational services either as part of the software offering in a traditional SaaS model or with a software vendor and systems integrator team depending on the relationship of the services component to the software vendor.
3. Scale assumptions: ~1.2 billion claims per year (for sizing) and ~33.6 million beneficiaries in Original Medicare. CMS prefers a single scalable instance of vendor software supporting all beneficiaries; however, load may be distributed across an instance per MAC or MAC region if analytics, editing, and real-time program integrity have real-time cross-region awareness.
4. Stakeholder engagement: The contractor(s) are expected to coordinate closely with CMS, MACs, and Providers in workgroups, demos, requirements gathering and validation, training, and communications facilitated by the vendor and contractors in partnership with CMS.
5. Incremental delivery: Phased deployments including incremental run-out based roll outs including focused workstreams with defined scope, resources and timelines. Vendor as well as independent validation of outputs compared against legacy systems running in parallel will provide objective evidence of parity and reduce risk.

The Centers for Medicare & Medicaid Services (CMS) seeks to replatform its Medicare Fee-for-Service (FFS) claims adjudication environment by replacing four legacy COBOL-based systems -- the Multi-Carrier System (MCS), Fiscal Intermediary Shared System (FISS), ViPS Medicare System (VMS), and the Common Working File (CWF). The ClaimsCore program will acquire a Commercial-Off-The-Shelf (COTS), configurable, interoperable claims processing platform capable of sub-second adjudication, improved real-time fraud prevention, and transparent interoperability through HIPAA X12 and FHIR/REST APIs. The program supports CMS strategic goals for cost deflation, transparency, and policy agility. This platform will form the basis for all future Medicare FFS claims processing and consolidate what are currently separate systems (Part A, Part B, DME) into a unified solution.

This procurement is for the proprietary, commercial off-the-shelf (COTS), brand-name Menlo Security, Inc.'s Cloud Browser (MSCB) product, as a Menlo Security, Inc. reseller-provided managed service, providing the Department of War's (DoW's) remote browsing isolation (RBI) solution for CBII. The action includes the renewal of existing subscription licenses, the procurement of new subscription licenses, and the incorporation of Menlo Security, Inc.'s Highly Evasive and Adaptive Threats (HEAT) Shield with Artificial Intelligence/Machine Learning (AI/ML) capabilities to enhance cybersecurity measures.

Since 2020, the DoW has leveraged CBII through the MSCB platform under a production Other Transaction Authority (OTA) agreement. Over the past five years, MSCB has continuously evolved to safeguard DoW users from cyber threat actors by proactively engineering defenses against malicious tactics, techniques, and procedures.

Originally hosted on Amazon Web Services, this capability has been further enhanced with the strategic expansion of MSCB to a second cloud environment, the Google Cloud Platform. This expansion improves system redundancy, optimizes load balancing, and ensures uninterrupted availability of the CBII environment, even during outages. MSCB guarantees continuous, secure web browsing for DoW personnel worldwide.

The CBII Program Management Office (PMO) projects the total user base under OPORD 21-0006 to range between 3M and 3.2M, with the capacity to scale up to 3.4M users to accommodate surge requirements under an enterprise licensing model.

BACKGROUND: Case Complete is a commercial off-the-shelf (COTS) workflow and case management application used to document and manage the internal SAPRO processes that ensure the reliability of DSAID data. While DSAID is the system of record for sexual assault incident data, Case Complete provides the dedicated technical solution to track DSAID-related requirements, decisions, and supporting documentation—functions that cannot be managed within the DSAID production system. c. Provide a dedicated technical solution to track DSAID-related requirements, decisions, and supporting documentation.
d. Provide a desktop client, mobile app, web client and web portal with SAPRO designated representative access to update content.

The system must be compatible with rat models and support auditory stimuli for evoked potential testing.

The scope of this effort is to acquire services or capabilities for AFE capabilities focused on features identified by FG as important but challenging. This includes the provisioning, configuration, deployment, and sustainment of a commercial/out-of-the-box AFE capability to automate the identification and extraction of geospatial features from imagery and other source materials. AFE capabilities will be a critical enabler for modernizing Foundation GEOINT processes and will be integrated with key NGA systems. The work will support NGA's maritime, aeronautical, topographic, geographic, and geomatics mission domains and will be integrated into NGA's FG production environments on all required security domains.

This RFI seeks industry input on available out-of-the-box AFE capabilities and how they can be provided as a service to support NGA operations to automatically detect and extract geospatial features from various data input sources, including imagery and raster maps, to meet the objectives described within the AFE Statement of Objectives (SOO). NGA envisions this initiative to leverage the use of artificial intelligence and machine learning (AI/ML) to transform geospatial intelligence production, significantly reducing manual processing time, increasing data accuracy, and enabling the rapid delivery of intelligence to decision-makers.

1. INTRODUCTION. The Contractor shall provide access to a commercial off the shelf (COTS) Human Performance Online Platform built to optimize athlete Human Performance, as defined in this Statement of Work (SOW), except for those items specified as government furnished property and services. The Contractor shall perform in accordance with (IAW) the terms and conditions of the contract and consistently with the established standards in this SOW and contract.

1.2. BACKGROUND. USASOC HPW Program requires an online platform that continues the current programming, data collection, and familiar user interface. The application must also connected mobile application for use by Human Performance professionals and Soldiers on-site and in the field. This platform would be used in support of optimizing Human Performance programs and Soldier exercise tracking, as well as can fully integrate via open API with electronic athlete management systems and import other data integration tools (i.e. various testing measures). Platform should allow for increased interface between Human Performance professionals and Soldiers, allow for large group Human Performance plan development, distribution and management, will gain efficiencies in manpower for Human Performance professionals, and allow for succinct data analysis for command and program level discussions regarding Human Performance programming within the multidisciplinary Human Performance domain.

3.1 Guardian Automation Layer (GAL): The GAL will serve as the Single User Interface for Guardians and HR Practitioners to interact with military HR Data. Historically, Guardian data has been stored within disparate systems, making process improvements and automation cumbersome. With no single source of truth for Guardian records or port-of-call for completing military HR actions, practitioners and end users rely on manual workarounds to reach data needed for decision making.

The primary purpose of the Space Force GAL is to track and manage Guardians in a service without component structure throughout the military HR lifecycle. The desired solution will allow the Guardian a continuous experience within the GAL throughout the entirety of the HR lifecycle, from onboarding and career development to retirement and beyond. The GAL will be flexible, with a positive user experience based in human-centered design practices that are reflective of modern commercial capabilities.

The USSF is pursuing a solution that leverages role-based authorities within a unified interface to analyze data and create interactive dashboards. Low code/no code configurability will allow practitioners to adapt to the evolving policy and process requirements, such as PMA.

Automation and AI capabilities are a highly desired function of the GAL. The USSF aims to greatly reduce administrative burden by freeing Guardians from routine and repetitive tasks and will allow for increased focus on higher level work to deliver results for the USSF. It will also improve data quality, timeliness of service delivery, Guardian self-service, and HR data transparency to Guardians.

In line with current USSF and DOW policies, the Government is seeking to minimize custom software development and maximize the use of commercial-off-the-shelf (COTS), software-as-a-service (SaaS), and configurable solutions for the GAL.

1 Vision
The ClaimsCore program seeks to re-platform Medicare Fee for Service claims processing by replacing legacy "Shared Systems" including the Multi-Carrier System (MCS), Fiscal Intermediary Shared System (FISS) and Durable Medical Equipment Claims System (DME) as well as the Common Working File (CWF) with a flexible and interoperable platform that reduces administrative burden and sets improvement of the beneficiary experience as the program's guiding objective. CMS intends to procure a Software as a Services (SaaS) Commercially-Available-Off-The-Shelf (COTS) product and complimentary configuration and integration services that will provide CMS with near real-time adjudication and claims status, policy agility, industry leading enterprise tools, improved fraud prevention capabilities, and tighter integration across CMS, Medicare Administrative Contractor (MACs), providers, and partners.

1.1 Objectives
1. Replace Shared Systems and the Common Working File (CWF): Replace FISS, MCS, DME, and CWF with an enterprise class, near real time platform while maintaining uninterrupted Medicare Fee for Service operations during transition.
2. Beneficiary Transparency and Timeliness: Enable near real-time adjudication and claims status, faster explanations of benefits, and better visibility into beneficiary responsibility.
3. Reduce Provider Burden: Reduce provider burden through increased transparency and consistency, increased automation, and improved revenue certainty.
4. Fraud, Waste and Abuse (FWA) reduction (i.e., Payment Integrity): Speed and flexibility in designing and implementing pre-payment risk scoring and edits, improved payment accuracy, auditing, and anomaly detection to prevent improper payments and protect beneficiaries and taxpayers.
5. Policy Agility and Flexibility: Configuration-driven rules to evaluate, back test, quantify impact, and implement new and alternative payment models without long development cycles.

4.1 Assumptions
1. Software Vendor: CMS intends to procure a SaaS based COTS platform, through a competitive process, to be configured and extended by the vendor in collaboration with CMS, MACs, and/or supporting contractors.
2. Professional Services: CMS envisions its primary role as policy development and oversight and expects to contract for operational services either as part of the software offering in a traditional SaaS model or with a software vendor and systems integrator team depending on the relationship of the services component to the software vendor.
3. Scale assumptions: ~1.2 billion claims per year (for sizing) and ~33.6 million beneficiaries in Original Medicare. CMS prefers a single scalable instance of vendor software supporting all beneficiaries; however, load may be distributed across an instance per MAC or MAC region if analytics, editing, and real-time program integrity have real-time cross-region awareness.
4. Stakeholder engagement: The contractor(s) are expected to coordinate closely with CMS, MACs, and Providers in workgroups, demos, requirements gathering and validation, training, and communications facilitated by the vendor and contractors in partnership with CMS.
5. Incremental delivery: Phased deployments including incremental run-out based roll outs including focused workstreams with defined scope, resources and timelines. Vendor as well as independent validation of outputs compared against legacy systems running in parallel will provide objective evidence of parity and reduce risk.
6. Warranty and Service Level Agreements: Contractor(s) must ensure appropriate system performance based on operational needs assessment and tied to payment penalties.

6 Challenge-Based Acquisition
ClaimsCore will be procured through a multi-phase challenge process. The objective is to create an environment where acquisition decisions are based on experimentation and demonstration of existing or emerging technology through a rapid and impactful assessment of commercial solutions and competing "prototypes" (i.e., limited implementations).

CMS contemplates multiple awards including a few contracts – perhaps as many as four or perhaps only one. Due to the operational cost and burden of a PoC, fewer awards are preferred. Accordingly, CMS has established an aggressive base-period (POC Phase) affordability objective. CMS encourages bidders to propose the best Proof of Concept solution possible at the lowest possible cost. Those vendors will begin work under a proof-of-concept (POC) phase where their solutions are tested against real CMS data and measured against CMS-established success criteria and metrics.

After the POC phase, CMS anticipates the down-select of contractors by not exercising option(s) for further work based on unilateral determination of CMS' best interest. This approach ensures solutions are validated in practice, stakeholder confidence is built incrementally, and only Contractors with proven capability at scale advance.
